channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draws on March 4 Have No Jackpot Winners
item.description: On Wednesday, March 4, 2026, the major lottery draws conducted by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) did not produce any jackpot winners. In the Megalotto 6/45, no player matched the win...

item.title: Texas Senate race pits Ken Paxton against John Cornyn in a high‑stakes GOP primary runoff
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton and longtime U.S. Senator John Cornyn are advancing to a May 26 primary runoff for the Republican nomination in Texas’s Senate race, setting up a bitter intra‑party s...

item.title: Female Engineer Killed by Former Partner in Catarman, Brother Injured
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Catarman, Northern Samar, where 25-year-old engineer Karen Grace Corsino was shot dead by her former partner, identified as Sgt. Jerome Lluz Tejero, an active member of t...

item.title: IS Sahel Kills Civilians in Western Niger Villages
item.description: For Niger, wahala don dey as Islamist armed group, Islamic State in the Sahel (IS Sahel), don kill 30 civilians including 4 pikin dem, plus 5 captured fighters, for two separate attacks wey happen sin...

item.title: Laura Anderson and Sam Gowland manage to leave Dubai amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Laura Anderson successfully secured a flight from Dubai to Edinburgh with her two-year-old daughter Bonnie amid escalating tensions in the Middle East, caused by a series of missile strikes and retali...

item.title: Challenges for the U.S. in Reducing China’s Control of Critical Mineral Supply Chains
item.description: The U.S. recently hosted a critical minerals summit aimed at reducing China's dominant role in the global supply of essential minerals used in smartphones, weapons systems, lithium-ion batteries, and ...

item.title: Congress announces first list of 42 candidates for Assam assembly elections, Gaurav Gogoi to contest from Jorhat
item.description: On March 3, 2026, the Congress party in Assam released its first list of 42 candidates for the upcoming elections to the 126-member Assam assembly. This makes Congress the first political party in the...

item.title: TikTok Confirms It Will Not Implement End-to-End Encryption for Direct Messages Over Safety Concerns
item.description: TikTok has decided not to introduce end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for direct messages on its platform, according to a report by the BBC. The company stated that implementing E2EE would make it more dif...

item.title: Examining the legality and implications of US-Israeli military actions against Iran
item.description: The article critiques the double standards often applied to international law, particularly in relation to military actions by powerful countries. It raises questions about the legality of former Pres...

item.title: Pakistani Parliamentary Leaders Briefed on Afghanistan Operation and Middle East Conflict, Call for National Unity
item.description: On March 4, 2026, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif chaired an in-camera briefing at the PM House for leaders of parliamentary parties on the evolving regional security landscape. The session, lasting ove...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens International Partnerships on Climate Change and Environment
item.description: On March 3, 2026, Tanzanian Minister of State in the Vice President’s Office for Union and Environment, Engineer Hamad Masauni, emphasized the country's commitment to addressing climate change and env...

item.title: Three arrested under National Security Act over alleged Chinese espionage activities
item.description: Three men have been arrested on suspicion of assisting a foreign intelligence service in an investigation linked to China, the Metropolitan Police have confirmed. The arrests were carried out on Wedne...

item.title: Heavy Rains Trigger Mudslides and Rockfalls in Elgeyo Marakwet, Killing at Least 10 with Rescue Efforts Hampered
item.description: Heavy rains that started on Friday evening have caused serious havoc in Elgeyo Marakwet County, especially along the steep Kerio Escarpment. Mudslides, rockfalls and flooding have hit several villages...

item.title: DA mocks Lesufi over hotel shower remark amid Gauteng water crisis
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) in Gauteng has launched a provocative billboard campaign targeting Premier Panyaza Lesufi over his remarks about the ongoing water crisis in parts of the province. The bil...

item.title: Fans Can Now Stay at Shane Hollander's Cottage Featured in 'Heated Rivalry'
item.description: Fans of the hit HBO Max series 'Heated Rivalry' now have the chance to stay at Shane Hollander's cottage, the location of a memorable romantic moment between hockey characters Ilya Rozanov and Shane H...

item.title: Allegations Surface Against Shina Philips Over Mismanagement Of Police Conference Funds Linked To Former IGP Egbetokun
item.description: Senior insiders for di Nigeria Police Force don accuse Mr. Shina Philips, wey be di Managing Director/CEO of Lenders Consult International and close ally to former Inspector-General of Police Kayode E...

item.title: Canadian Gas Prices Rise Amid Iran Conflict, More Increases Expected
item.description: Gasoline prices in Canada have started climbing, with an average increase of nine cents per litre since the conflict with Iran entered its fifth day. Dan McTeague, former Liberal MP and president of C...

item.title: Indoor Candle Burning Contributes to Harmful Air Pollution
item.description: During the winter months, many people use candles to create a warm and cosy indoor atmosphere. In Denmark, around 75% of the population burns candles at least twice a week, with 34% using them daily d...

item.title: Michelin-Starred Chef Jean Delport Shares Insights on Great British Menu, Michelin Pressures, Customer Habits, and British Cuisine
item.description: In a monthly Cheap Eats interview with Sky News, Michelin-starred chef Jean Delport of Restaurant Interlude in West Sussex discusses his experience competing on last year's Great British Menu. He desc...

item.title: Rising Tensions in the Middle East: US-Iran Conflict Escalates, Iran to Appoint New Supreme Leader
item.description: The Middle East is experiencing a rapid escalation in hostilities following a US-Israeli strike on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran has vowed retaliation, with its judiciar...
